channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sacramento Officials Discuss Meeting Disruptions After Chaos
item.description: During a heated City Hall meeting in Sacramento, mayor Kevin McCarty and vice mayor Karina Talamantes were caught on a live microphone discussing potential measures to address audience disruptions aft...

item.title: 19th-century travelogue details a hermit's pilgrimage to Himalayan sacred sites
item.description: This excerpt from 'The Forest of Snow' by Swami Ramananda Bharati recounts a 19th-century hermit's journey from Joshimath in the Garhwal Himalayas to the sacred sites of Mount Kailash and Manas Sarova...

item.title: Pakistan's Military Leadership Reforms: New CNSC Commander Appointed
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has announced the promotion of Lieutenant General Aamer Raza, the chief of general staff of the Pakistan Army, to the rank of a four-star general and his appointment as t...

item.title: PM Modi announces proposed Fast-Track Courts and stricter penalties to tackle examination paper leaks
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi said that examination paper leaks are a serious concern affecting lakhs of students and their families, and assured the country that the Union government will introduce st...

item.title: ASEAN Urges Open Straits Amid US-Iran Tensions
item.description: The Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) has called for the maintenance of open straits amid escalating tensions between the United States and Iran, which have cast a shadow over regional st...

item.title: Ted Cruz Accuses Nigeria of Using Lobbyists to Conceal Christian Killings and Insecurity
item.description: Senator Ted Cruz of the United States has accused Nigeria's President Bola Tinubu government of using public relations lobbying to hide the killing of Christians in the country. Cruz criticized Nigeri...

item.title: CASA investigation reveals pilot's reckless conduct in fatal 2019 helicopter crash
item.description: Seven years after a near-death helicopter crash involving high-profile tourism operator Troy Thomas, the Civil Aviation Safety Authority (CASA) has concluded an investigation finding the pilot's actio...

item.title: Merebank NPO supports girls' education through sanitary pad distribution
item.description: The Merebank-based non-profit organisation Save the Dignity of Our School Girls has distributed over 30,000 sanitary pads to empower girls to stay in school and combat menstrual stigma. Founded in 202...

item.title: North Bay mayor and Jewish group condemn 'Bigotfest' event amid concerns over hate ideology
item.description: The mayor of North Bay and a Jewish advocacy organization have condemned a planned weekend gathering dubbed 'Bigotfest' by organizers, who describe it as a 'family fun for all' event. The event, sched...

item.title: CBI finds no evidence linking Sanjeev Mukhiya to 2024 NEET-UG paper leak
item.description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has concluded that there is no evidence to implicate Sanjeev Mukhiya, the main suspect in the 2024 NEET-UG paper leak case, in the theft or distribution of th...

item.title: Aliyah Boston and Tre-Vaughn Minott End Relationship After Secret Split
item.description: Aliyah Boston and Tre-Vaughn Minott, both basketball players, have officially ended their relationship. Aliyah clarified on Instagram that she is single and no longer dating Tre-Vaughn, despite no pub...

item.title: Young Artist Redefining Zanzibar's Art Scene with Unique Fusion
item.description: Callyson Donald, known as Callyfits, is a young artist revitalizing Zanzibar's aging Stone Town art scene. His journey began after his mother's death, where he turned to art to earn a living. Combinin...

item.title: South Africa Faces Economic Risks from Declining Mozambique Gas Supplies
item.description: The article highlights the impending decline in natural gas supplies from Mozambique's Pande and Temane fields, set to begin in 2028, and its potential impact on South Africa's economy and industrial ...

item.title: Pakistan Army Chief Promoted to Strategic Command Role
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has announced the promotion of Lieutenant General Aamer Raza to the rank of four-star general and his appointment as Commander National Strategic Command (CNSC). This mov...

item.title: Bill on Social Media Offices Sparks Debate Among Stakeholders
item.description: The Nigerian Senate's proposed bill requiring social media platforms to establish physical offices in the country has sparked debate among stakeholders. While some, like the Chairman of the Practition...

item.title: Tropical Storm Kiyapo Approaches Cagayan, Possible Landfall Expected
item.description: Tropical Storm Kiyapo (Noul) intensified further on Friday, July 24, 2026, with maximum sustained winds increasing to 75 km/h. The storm is expected to make landfall in the Babuyan Islands, prompting ...

item.title: Trump's Condition on Saudi Nuclear Deal Sparks Uncertainty Over Agreement
item.description: President Donald Trump has introduced a new condition for the recently announced civilian nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, requiring the kingdom to recognize Israel. This requirement has cast doubt ove...

item.title: Sara Duterte Urges LGUs to Enforce Building Codes and Improve Emergency Protocols
item.description: Sara Duterte, the Vice President of the Philippines, has called on local government units (LGUs) to strictly enforce building codes and improve emergency protocols to enhance public safety. The direct...

item.title: Two Men Discover They Were Switched at Birth After DNA Test Reveals Family Mix-Up
item.description: A North Dakota man named Kyle Bylin received a home DNA test as a gift and discovered he had no genetic link to the family that raised him. The test led him to Jeremy Morrison, confirming they were sw...

item.title: Key Tech and Consumer Stocks Move Amid Market Volatility
item.description: The stock market experienced notable fluctuations on July 23, 2026, as investors closely monitored major tech and consumer companies. Alphabet (GOOGL), Tesla (TSLA), Nestlé (NESN), and Intel (INTC) sa...
